key_insight: A SCAPS-1D plus machine-learning workflow can rapidly rank multilayer perovskite solar-cell stacks and uncover high-efficiency, lead-free architectures without exhaustive screening.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a practical design map for choosing ETL, absorber, and HTL combinations to boost power conversion efficiency while supporting environmentally friendly perovskite device development.
method: Trained an ML model on a representative subset to predict PCE for unexplored stacks.
Used SHAP to identify the most influential descriptors and validate top candidates with full device simulations.
evidence_signal: Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation showed reliable ranking performance, and the best verified devices reached 28.85% PCE for FTO/TiO2/Cs2AgBiBr6/NiO/Ag and 28.62% for FTO/SnO2/Cs2AgInBr6/NiO/Ag.
watch_item: Predictions depend on the quality and coverage of the SCAPS training subset, so out-of-distribution material stacks may still require full physical validation.
paper_title: Multilayer Screening of Double and Conventional Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D and Machine Learning: Optimization of ETL, HTL, and Absorber for High-Efficiency Architectures
